workflow: RupeeKit Workflow 1 - Keyword Research and Content Plan

plan.searchIntent: Help salaried employees calculate possible HRA exemption using salary, rent, city type, and HRA received.
plan.targetPersona: Salaried employees in India seeking to optimize their tax savings through HRA exemption and understand the rules.
plan.primaryGoal: To provide an accurate and easy-to-use HRA exemption calculator for Indian salaried employees, helping them estimate their tax-exempt HRA amount and understand the underlying rules as per Indian income tax laws.
titleOptions: HRA Exemption Calculator India - Calculate Your Tax Savings
Calculate HRA Exemption in India | RupeeKit
India HRA Exemption Calculator for Salaried Employees
plan.recommendedMetaTitle: HRA Exemption Calculator India | Calculate Tax Savings
plan.recommendedMetaDescription: Calculate HRA exemption in India using salary, HRA received, rent paid, and metro or non-metro city rules.

inputs.name: dearnessAllowance
inputs.label: Dearness Allowance (DA) (Monthly)
inputs.helpText: Your monthly Dearness Allowance, if applicable and part of salary for retirement benefits.

inputs.name: hraReceived
inputs.label: HRA Received (Monthly)
inputs.helpText: The actual House Rent Allowance you receive from your employer monthly.

inputs.name: rentPaid
inputs.label: Rent Paid (Monthly)
inputs.helpText: The actual monthly rent you pay for your accommodation.

inputs.name: cityType
inputs.label: City Type
inputs.helpText: Select 'Metro' if you live in Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, or Chennai. Otherwise, select 'Non-Metro'.

calculatorSpec.formulaPlainEnglish: The HRA exemption is the least of the following three amounts: 1. Actual HRA received from your employer. 2. 50% of (Basic Salary + Dearness Allowance) if you live in a metro city (Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai), or 40% of (Basic Salary + Dearness Allowance) if you live in a non-metro city. 3. Actual rent paid minus 10% of (Basic Salary + Dearness Allowance). The remaining HRA, if any, is taxable.
calculatorSpec.formula: salaryForHRA = basicSalary + dearnessAllowance; condition2Percentage = (cityType === 'metro') ? 0.50 : 0.40; condition1 = hraReceived; condition2 = condition2Percentage * salaryForHRA; condition3 = rentPaid - (0.10 * salaryForHRA); hraExemption = Math.min(condition1, condition2, condition3); taxableHRA = hraReceived - hraExemption;

contentBrief.introAngle: Start by highlighting the significant tax-saving potential of HRA exemption for salaried individuals in India and introduce the calculator as a quick, reliable tool to estimate this benefit.
mustCover: What is HRA and its purpose?
Eligibility criteria for claiming HRA exemption (e.g., salaried, paying rent, not owning a house in the same city)
Detailed explanation of the three conditions for HRA exemption calculation.
Definition of 'Salary' for HRA purposes (Basic + DA + Commission if fixed percentage of turnover).
What constitutes a 'Metro City' for HRA calculation.
Documents required for claiming HRA (rent receipts, landlord's PAN if rent > ₹1 Lakh/year).
Scenarios where HRA exemption cannot be claimed (e.g., paying rent to spouse/parents, but not actually living there; living in own house).
Common mistakes to avoid when claiming HRA.
How to declare HRA in income tax returns (ITR).
contentBrief.exampleScenario: Priya lives in Bengaluru (non-metro) and pays ₹18,000 rent. Her monthly basic salary is ₹60,000, DA is ₹5,000, and HRA received is ₹25,000. Calculate her HRA exemption.
faqQuestions: Can I claim HRA if I pay rent to my parents?
What documents do I need to claim HRA exemption?
Is HRA exemption applicable if I own a house but live on rent in another city?
What happens if my HRA received is less than the rent I pay?
Do I need my landlord's PAN card to claim HRA?
Can both husband and wife claim HRA exemption if they live together and both pay rent?
trustNotes: Uses commonly followed Indian HRA exemption calculation rules for educational purposes.
Explains assumptions clearly so users can understand how the estimate is calculated.
Designed as an informational calculator, not as personalized tax or financial advice.
Users should verify final tax treatment with official guidance or a qualified tax professional.
contentBrief.disclaimer: This calculator is for educational and informational purposes only. It provides an estimate based on the inputs and assumptions shown. It is not tax, legal, investment, or financial advice. Please verify with official sources or consult a qualified professional before making tax or financial decisions.
